EnterpriseSDK
Table of Contents

Interface IAffineTransformation2D

Namespace
ESRI.ArcGIS.Geometry
Assembly
ESRI.Server.Geometry.dll

Provides access to members that define and manipulate affine transformations.

[InterfaceType(ComInterfaceType.InterfaceIsIUnknown)]
[Guid("D12F81B4-D263-11D2-9F29-00C04F6BDD7F")]
public interface IAffineTransformation2D : ITransformation

Properties

IsReflective

Indicates if the transformation contains a reflection (determinant is negative).

bool IsReflective { get; }

Property Value

bool

Examples

Sample Snippet

MoveOrigin

The origin of accumulated transformations used when projecting an affine transformation to a different spatial reference system.

IPoint MoveOrigin { set; }

Property Value

IPoint

Examples

Sample Snippet

Rotation

The rotation angle. Will not be able if different x/y scale factors have been incorporated into the transformation.

double Rotation { get; }

Property Value

double

Examples

Sample Snippet

SpatialReference

The spatial reference in which this transformation is meaningful.

ISpatialReference SpatialReference { get; set; }

Property Value

ISpatialReference

Examples

Sample Snippet

XScale

The scale along the X axis.

double XScale { get; }

Property Value

double

Examples

Sample Snippet

XTranslation

The translation along the X axis.

double XTranslation { get; }

Property Value

double

Examples

Sample Snippet

YScale

The scale along the Y axis.

double YScale { get; }

Property Value

double

Examples

Sample Snippet

YTranslation

The translation along the Y axis.

double YTranslation { get; }

Property Value

double

Examples

Sample Snippet

Methods

DefineFromControlPoints(int, ref IPoint, ref IPoint)

Defines the best affine transformation between two sets of points. Can be used to register paper maps on a digitizer.

void DefineFromControlPoints(int numPoints, ref IPoint fromPoints, ref IPoint toPoints)

Parameters

numPoints int
fromPoints IPoint
toPoints IPoint

Examples

Sample Snippet

DefineFromControlPointsEx(int, ref WKSPoint, ref WKSPoint) 

void DefineFromControlPointsEx(int numPoints, ref WKSPoint fromPoints, ref WKSPoint toPoints)

Parameters

numPoints int
fromPoints WKSPoint
toPoints WKSPoint

DefineFromEnvelopes(IEnvelope, IEnvelope) 

void DefineFromEnvelopes(IEnvelope from, IEnvelope to)

Parameters

from IEnvelope
to IEnvelope

DefineFromEnvelopesEx(IEnvelope, IEnvelope, IEnvelope, bool, bool, bool) 

void DefineFromEnvelopesEx(IEnvelope from, IEnvelope to, IEnvelope outFrom, bool assumeFalseOrigin, bool keepAspect, bool flipIt)

Parameters

from IEnvelope
to IEnvelope
outFrom IEnvelope
assumeFalseOrigin bool
keepAspect bool
flipIt bool

DefineReflection(ILine) 

void DefineReflection(ILine l)

Parameters

l ILine

GetControlPointError(int, out double, out double)

Returns the errors involved in moving control point i from the 'from' to 'to' system. These error terms are valid after using DefineFromControlPoints/Ex to define the transformation.

void GetControlPointError(int i, out double fromError, out double toError)

Parameters

i int
fromError double
toError double

Examples

Sample Snippet

GetRMSError(out double, out double)

RMS (Root Mean Square) error expressed relative to the 'from' and 'to' points defining the transformation. These error terms are valid after using DefineFromControlPoints/Ex to define the transformation.

void GetRMSError(out double fromError, out double toError)

Parameters

fromError double
toError double

Examples

Sample Snippet

Move(double, double)

Incorporates a translation factor into the transformation.

void Move(double dx, double dy)

Parameters

dx double
dy double

movementVector ILine

Incorporates a rotation (in radians) into the transformation.
